notes: SHIP v1.14.294 - Ship 4 design-call #4 (owner-confirmed specifics in chat): hero fault MAG STRIP + BLOCKER-verb accent. This is the LAST Command-Center Ship-4 call. (A) STATE-DRIVEN FAULT STRIP: new #cc-magstrip element in the hero .lens (between the ON SHIFT pill and the SCAN/LOG/BLOCKER verb row), rendered by cmd_render ONLY when the aggregate blockerCount>0 (blockerCount already computed at L19550-54 across all deployments; no new plumbing). Copy: '(warn) N BLOCKER[S] · TAP TO REVIEW' (singular '1 BLOCKER'); the warn glyph is U+26A0 + U+FE0E text-presentation request so it inherits the mag color (see DEVICE-VERIFY - iOS may still force the yellow emoji). Taps to cmd_route('work','issues') - the same blockers/issues door the BLOCKERS stat card uses; only shown when count>0 so never a dead tap. Honest-state: hidden at rung A/B and whenever zero blockers. (B) BLOCKER-VERB ACCENT: the hero BLOCKER verb (.hverb) gets a persistent .hverb-blk flat MAG identity tint (border rgba(255,43,214,.35), bg rgba(255,43,214,.06), text #ff2bd6) - SCAN/LOG stay cyan. ALWAYS-ON per owner (identity: mag=the fault action, per R5), FLAT tint not a glow - respects the .292 one-emitter rule (identity, not decoration). All fault styling uses the established .290 house language (#ff2bd6 / rgba(255,43,214,x), flat borders+bg, no bloom). CSS-only + one small cmd_render toggle; no data paths touched. Three-stamp .293->.294; braces 11748->11755 balanced (4 CSS rules + 3 JS braces); CRLF preserved. DEVICE-VERIFY (John): with an active deployment carrying blockers, the hero shows a mag '(warn) N BLOCKERS · TAP TO REVIEW' strip under ON SHIFT; tap opens the issues/blockers list; strip VANISHES when blockers hit 0 (and at cold rung A/B). The BLOCKER verb reads mag always (SCAN/LOG cyan). WATCH: does the (warn) glyph render mag-monochrome or as a yellow iOS emoji? - if emoji, easy swap. Ship 4 is COMPLETE (calls #1 .291, #2 .292, #3 .293, #4 .294). NEXT: CLEAN window (askrow CSS tombstones from .293 + R3 BOM tombstones), then N-09 (FORGE aisle door) + N-06 (Rail Compass) per the EXECUTE-ORDER chain.
